title: Reorder init script to run before tmux in yaam new (spec: docs/specs/03-init-before-tmux.md)
Update tests for session-name-as-branch: drop prefix from helpers, add test_new_branch_matches_session_name, remove test_load_defaults_branch_prefix — see docs/specs/05-session-name-as-branch.md
Update README and example.toml to reflect init-before-tmux order (spec: docs/specs/03-init-before-tmux.md)
Stage 5: Session state
description: Persistent mapping between agent names, worktrees, tmux panes, and the profile used to create them.

title: Implement AgentSession Pydantic model
description: AgentSession model: name, branch, profile_name, worktree_path, tmux_session, tmux_pane_ref, created_at, status.

title: Implement SessionStore (JSON state file)
description: SessionStore: read/write JSON state file at ~/.config/agent/sessions.json. Methods: add, get, list, remove, update_status. Use file lock for concurrent writes.

title: Implement AgentConfig model and config loader
description: AgentConfig model: default_profile, tmux_session_name, state_file_path. Load from ~/.config/agent/config.toml with sensible defaults.

title: Stage 3: Profile system
description: A named profile config that fully describes an agent role — its repo, tmux layout, and init sequence. Profiles live at ~/.config/agent/profiles/<name>.toml.

title: Implement AgentProfile Pydantic model
description: AgentProfile model with fields: name, description, repo_path, default_branch_prefix, tmux_setup_script, init_script, init_env.

title: Implement profile load() and list_profiles()
description: load(name: str) -> AgentProfile reads ~/.config/agent/profiles/<name>.toml. list_profiles() -> list[AgentProfile] lists all available profiles.

title: Implement profile validate()
description: validate(profile: AgentProfile) -> list[str]. Checks scripts exist and are executable, repo path exists. Raises ProfileValidationError for non-executable scripts.

title: Add agent profile list subcommand
description: Renders a rich table of available profiles with description.

title: Add agent profile validate subcommand
description: Runs validate() on a named profile and reports issues.

title: Write example.toml profile on first run
description: Write an example.toml profile to ~/.config/agent/profiles/ on first run.

title: Stage 7: Attach and sync
description: agent attach and agent sync for day-to-day use. Detect orphaned sessions and untracked worktrees.

title: Implement agent attach command
description: Switch the current terminal to the named tmux pane. Print error if pane is dead.

title: Implement agent sync command
description: Find orphaned sessions (in store but pane dead/worktree gone). Find untracked worktrees (wt list entries not in store). Report discrepancies with rich table. --fix flag to auto-remove orphaned sessions.

title: Stage 9: Polish and packaging
description: Distributable, documented tool ready for daily use. JSON output, shell completions, README, agent doctor, PyPI packaging.

title: Add --json output flag to agent list
description: Add --json flag to agent list for scripting use cases.

title: Add shell completions via typer
description: agent --install-completion for zsh and bash shell completions.

title: Write README.md
description: Installation, quickstart, profile authoring guide, and Worktrunk setup instructions.

title: Implement agent doctor command
description: Check wt installed, tmux running, config valid, at least one profile valid.

title: Package for distribution (PyPI or pyinstaller)
description: Publish to PyPI or package as standalone binary. pip install agent-cli should produce a working agent binary.

title: Stage 6: Core commands
description: agent new, agent list, and agent kill working end to end, profile-driven. Full spawn sequence: load profile -> create worktree -> run tmux setup -> run init script -> persist session.

title: Implement agent new command with full spawn sequence
description: agent new <name> --profile <profile> [--branch <branch>]. Spawn sequence: 1) Load/validate profile, 2) worktrunk.create, 3) tmux.run_setup_script, 4) init.run, 5) persist AgentSession. Stream init output with rich spinner.

title: Implement init.py — post-init script runner
description: run(script_path, repo_path, worktree_path, env) -> None. Calls init script with repo_path as $1 and worktree_path as $2. Merges profile.init_env. Streams stdout/stderr. Raises InitScriptError on non-zero exit. Logs to ~/.config/agent/logs/<name>-init.log.

title: Implement agent list command
description: Read session store, cross-reference with wt list and tmux.pane_alive. Render rich table: name, profile, branch, status, age, pane.

title: Implement agent kill command
description: Kill tmux pane, run wt remove on worktree, remove session from store. Clean up on failure. Print helpful error for unknown names.

title: Add cleanup on spawn failure
description: If init script or tmux setup fails during agent new, clean up the worktree and tmux pane. Surface the log path and exit cleanly.

title: Stage 2: Worktrunk wrapper
description: Python wrapper around wt CLI commands, with structured return types.

title: Implement worktrunk.create() — call wt switch --create
description: Implement create(branch: str, repo_path: str) -> WorktreeInfo. Calls wt switch --create <branch> from repo_path.

title: Implement worktrunk.remove() — call wt remove
description: Implement remove(worktree_path: str) -> None. Calls wt remove from the worktree path.

title: Implement worktrunk.list() — parse wt list output
description: Implement list(repo_path: str) -> list[WorktreeInfo]. Calls wt list and parses output.

title: Implement worktrunk.merge() — call wt merge
description: Implement merge(worktree_path: str, target: str = 'main') -> None. Calls wt merge <target>.

title: Define WorktreeInfo Pydantic model
description: Define WorktreeInfo Pydantic model with fields: branch, path, status, head.

title: Add wt_available() check and WorktrunkNotFoundError
description: Implement wt_available() -> bool check on startup. Raise WorktrunkNotFoundError when wt is not installed.

title: Add unit tests for worktrunk module with subprocess mocking
description: All worktrunk commands covered by unit tests using subprocess mocking.

title: Stage 4: tmux wrapper
description: Python wrapper around libtmux for managing panes tied to agent sessions, including execution of profile-defined layout scripts.

title: Implement tmux.get_or_create_session()
description: get_or_create_session(name: str) -> libtmux.Session. Handle the case where tmux is not running.

title: Implement tmux.run_setup_script()
description: run_setup_script(script_path: str, worktree_path: str) -> None. Calls the profile's tmux setup script with worktree_path as $1. Captures stderr and raises TmuxScriptError on non-zero exit.

title: Implement tmux pane management (create, send_keys, kill, alive)
description: Implement create_pane, send_keys, kill_pane, pane_alive. Define PaneRef dataclass with session_id, window_id, pane_id. All operations must be idempotent.

title: Add per-session log file for tmux setup script output
description: Capture tmux setup script output in a log file per session for debugging.

title: Stage 1: Project scaffold
description: Runnable CLI with no real functionality yet. Just the skeleton. Entry point, package structure, ruff config, and basic imports.

title: Initialize project with pyproject.toml using uv init
description: Set up the project with uv init, configure pyproject.toml with package metadata.

title: Add core dependencies: typer, rich, pydantic, libtmux
description: Add dependencies via uv add: typer, rich, pydantic, libtmux.

title: Create package structure (cli.py, config.py, profile.py, session.py, tmux.py, worktrunk.py)
description: Create agent/ package with stub modules: cli.py (typer app entry point), config.py (user config model), profile.py (AgentProfile model + loader stub), session.py (AgentSession model + state file), tmux.py (libtmux wrapper stub), worktrunk.py (wt subprocess wrapper stub).

title: Configure bundled profiles/ directory in package data
description: Ensure the bundled profiles/ directory is included in package data. Configure pyproject.toml [tool.hatch.build.targets.wheel] include or equivalent.

title: Configure ruff for linting and formatting
description: Configure ruff in pyproject.toml: linting (ruff check) and formatting (ruff format) replacing both flake8 and black.

title: Register agent as CLI entry point
description: Register agent as a CLI entry point in pyproject.toml. Confirm agent --help runs cleanly.

title: Stage 10: Rename to yaam
description: Rename the package, executable, Python module, and all config paths from `agent`/`agent-cli` to `yaam`. Spec: docs/specs/02-rename-to-yaam.md

title: Update pyproject.toml for yaam rename
description: Update pyproject.toml: rename [project] name to 'yaam', update [project.scripts] entries (agent→yaam, agent-worker→yaam-worker), update wheel packages path (src/agent→src/yaam), update ruff known-first-party. See docs/specs/02-rename-to-yaam.md §1.

title: Rename src/agent/ to src/yaam/ and update all imports
description: Rename the src/agent/ directory to src/yaam/. Update all internal imports from 'from agent.' to 'from yaam.' and 'import agent.' to 'import yaam.' across the entire package. See docs/specs/02-rename-to-yaam.md §2.

title: Update config and state paths from ~/.config/agent/ to ~/.config/yaam/
description: Update all hardcoded path constants in config.py, session.py, profile.py, and init.py from ~/.config/agent/ to ~/.config/yaam/. Affects: profiles dir, sessions.json, logs dir, config.toml. See docs/specs/02-rename-to-yaam.md §3.

title: Update test suite for yaam rename
description: Update all test files: replace 'from agent.' with 'from yaam.' imports, update CLI runner invocations where app name is referenced, update any path assertions referencing ~/.config/agent/. Covers tests/test_*.py. See docs/specs/02-rename-to-yaam.md §5.

title: Update docs and AGENTS.md for yaam rename
description: Update README.md (title, pip install yaam, all shell examples agent→yaam, config paths), AGENTS.md (project overview, repo structure, config paths, command examples), and docs/specs/01-init.md (command examples, package/module name refs). See docs/specs/02-rename-to-yaam.md §§6,7,8.

title: Remove default_branch_prefix from AgentProfile and cli.py branch logic — see docs/specs/05-session-name-as-branch.md
Pass session name as $1 to tmux setup script (worktree path shifts to $2) — see docs/specs/04-tmux-script-session-arg.md
Update README, AGENTS.md, and create-profile skill to remove default_branch_prefix — see docs/specs/05-session-name-as-branch.md
Update README and AGENTS.md to document new tmux setup script args ($1=session, $2=worktree) — see docs/specs/04-tmux-script-session-arg.md
Stage 8: LangGraph orchestrator
description: A supervisor that dispatches tasks to worker agents, each spawned via a named profile. Multi-agent orchestration via LangGraph.

title: Add LangGraph and langchain-anthropic dependencies
description: Add langgraph and langchain-anthropic via uv add.

title: Define OrchestratorState and Task models
description: Task TypedDict: id, description, profile. OrchestratorState TypedDict: tasks, agents, results, phase (planning/executing/reviewing/done).

title: Implement orchestrator graph nodes
description: Implement orchestrator/graph.py with nodes: plan_node (break goal into tasks, assign profiles), dispatch_node (call agent new per task), monitor_node (poll pane status), collect_node (read results/<name>.json), review_node (supervisor reviews results).

title: Implement orchestrator worker wrapper
description: orchestrator/worker.py — thin wrapper each agent process runs. Writes result to results/<name>.json on completion.

title: Add agent run CLI command
description: agent run <goal> [--profile <profile>] — entry point into the LangGraph orchestrator.
